meta.purpose: NORS complaint-coding training sources, ambiguity rules, and call-light/call-bell keyword routing refinements
meta.normalization_note: The returned research used F06 for call-light response examples. This application's active NORS hierarchy and Table 2 working data identify response to requests for assistance as F02, so call-light/call-bell response routing is normalized to F02 here.

source_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Basic Principles
source_rows.description: NORC Part II complaint-coding principles, including one complaint equals one complaint code, A-M versus N-Q code structure, and abuse/gross neglect/exploitation coding.

source_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Quiz
source_rows.description: NORC Part II quiz scenarios for practicing selection of the single best complaint code using Table 2.

source_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Quiz Answer Sheet
source_rows.description: Answer sheet reinforcing one complaint equals one complaint code and use of Table 2 examples and reporting tips.

source_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Beyond the Basics Quiz Answers
source_rows.description: Updated Beyond the Basics answer sheet with multi-issue scenario coding, K/L outside-agency examples, and barriers-to-transition examples.

source_rows.title: NORS Training Part II: Complaint Coding Webinar Slides
source_rows.description: Slides covering NORS complaint-coding basic principles and facility versus non-facility complaint structure.

source_rows.title: NORS Frequently Asked Questions: Complaint Coding
source_rows.description: NORC FAQ addressing one-code versus multiple distinct complaints and when to separate complaint issues.

coding_principle_rows.principle_text: One complaint equals one complaint code; select the single complaint code that best describes each complaint.
coding_principle_rows.practical_tool_rule: When parsing user text, group facts into distinct complaint issues and avoid assigning multiple similar codes for the same problem.

coding_principle_rows.principle_text: If a case involves multiple distinct complaints, identify codes for each complaint rather than forcing all issues into one code.
coding_principle_rows.practical_tool_rule: When a phrase bundle mentions unrelated problems, the tool should propose separate likely codes for review.

coding_principle_rows.principle_text: Use A-M codes for complaints against the facility and N-Q codes for complaints not against the facility.
coding_principle_rows.practical_tool_rule: Outside-agency or system concerns should not be forced into facility F-tag mappings when the facility is not the actor.

coding_principle_rows.principle_text: Use A-series codes only when the complaint meets definitions for abuse, gross neglect, or exploitation.
coding_principle_rows.practical_tool_rule: Ordinary poor care, delays, or rude tone should usually route to F-series care or D-series rights unless abuse/gross neglect facts are clear.

coding_principle_rows.principle_text: When a complaint could fit under two similar NORS codes, choose the one code that best describes the complaint using Table 2 examples and reporting tips.
coding_principle_rows.practical_tool_rule: The tool should present a primary likely code and simpler secondary review notes for overlapping scenarios.

ambiguity_rule_rows.primary_interpretation: Delayed response to resident requests for assistance, especially toileting, transfer, repositioning, or other care needs.
ambiguity_rule_rows.do_not_map_when: Do not treat every mention of a call bell as an equipment/device issue. If the complaint is that staff did not respond, F02 is the better primary route in this app's NORS hierarchy.

ambiguity_rule_rows.phrase_pattern: room change|moved rooms|moved to another room|lost my room
ambiguity_rule_rows.primary_interpretation: Room location or roommate issues within the facility, not facility discharge.

keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: NORS training uses call light and toileting examples as care-response issues. Under this app's active NORS hierarchy, response to call lights/call bells routes to F02 unless context shows a broken device, broad staffing complaint, retaliation, or extreme harm.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: If the narrative shows very long unattended periods, repeated serious harm, or staff intentionally denying help, flag for human review to consider A05 or another code.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: call bell not answered|call light not answered|no one answered call light|no one answered call bell|call bell ignored|call light ignored|unanswered call light|unanswered call bell|no one came
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: Unanswered call lights and call bells are treated as response-to-assistance care concerns. Staffing can be a related issue if the text emphasizes short staffing, but the immediate resident impact remains the primary route.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: If the text emphasizes retaliation after a complaint, review D06. If the text emphasizes short staffing as the complaint itself, review J03 as a related code.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: waited for toilet help|waited too long for toilet|no one helped me to the bathroom|kept waiting for help
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: Toileting delays and being left wet after a request for help are care-response concerns. Repeated severe harm may require human review for gross neglect.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Consider A05 only when the pattern is extreme, dangerous, or meets gross-neglect criteria; otherwise keep the care-response code as primary.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: short staffed and no one answers call bells|short staffed and call lights unanswered|not enough staff to answer call lights|not enough staff to answer call bells
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: When call-light delay is tied to staffing, F02 remains the primary care-response issue and J03 is a related staffing/system concern.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Use J03 as primary only when the complaint is mainly about overall staffing adequacy rather than a specific resident request for help.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: call bell broken|call light broken|call bell not working|call light not working|call light malfunction|call bell malfunction
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: A broken or malfunctioning call device is an equipment/access problem. If the broken device caused unanswered requests or delayed care, F02 should also be reviewed.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: Confirm whether the concern is the device itself or staff response after the resident requested help.

keyword_routing_rows.phrase_pattern: call bell out of reach|call light out of reach|could not reach call bell|could not reach call light
keyword_routing_rows.explanation_for_trace: A call device left out of reach prevents the resident from requesting assistance. This is primarily a care-response/setup issue, with equipment/access as a related review item.
keyword_routing_rows.warning_or_human_review_note: If staff intentionally removed access to the call bell or repeated failures caused serious harm, flag for human review.

tooltip_rows.label: Call Lights - Care vs Staffing
tooltip_rows.short_help: Unanswered call lights are usually a response-to-assistance care complaint.
tooltip_rows.details: NORS complaint-coding training uses call light and toileting scenarios as care complaints. In this app's NORS hierarchy, call light/call bell response routes to F02. Use staffing as related context when short staffing is prominent, and use equipment/device routing when the call bell itself is broken or out of reach.

tooltip_rows.label: Room Change vs Discharge
tooltip_rows.short_help: Room moves inside the facility are room issues, not discharge.
tooltip_rows.details: If a resident is moved to a different room or gets a new roommate but is not told to leave the facility, use the room-issues code. Use discharge codes only when the complaint involves being required to leave the facility or receiving a discharge/eviction notice.

tooltip_rows.label: Abuse vs Poor Care
tooltip_rows.short_help: Use A-codes only when abuse, gross neglect, or exploitation definitions are met.
tooltip_rows.details: Many complaints describe poor care, delays, or disrespect that should route to care or rights codes. A-codes should be reserved for facts supporting abuse, gross neglect, or exploitation.

human_review_flags.issue: Exact question numbers for several training examples were not included in the research handoff.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Before treating scenario rows as official question-level citations, a reviewer should open the full Part II quiz, answer sheet, and Table 2 to confirm exact example codes and question numbers.

human_review_flags.issue: Batch 10 research used F06 for call-light response, but this app's active NORS hierarchy uses F02.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Keep call-light/call-bell response routing normalized to F02 unless the project updates the full NORS hierarchy and crosswalk catalog to a different official code set.
